Requirement: System P1 outputs square wave to speaker
System P2 outputs a sine wave using 6-bit R-2R DAC with 64-sample sine lookup table
Notes: Music module exists but 64-sample sine LUT not implemented

Requirement: Piano mode: Press and hold C, D, E, F to play corresponding note continuously without gaps; stops immediately when button released
Notes: GPIO input drives continuous tone output

Requirement: Auto-Play Mode: plays one of three songs (Mary Had a Little Lamb, Twinkle Twinkle Little Star, Happy Birthday) in a loop until song is changed
Switch 1 toggles between Piano mode and Auto-Play mode
Switch 2 changes octave in Piano mode in round-robin order (Lower C → Middle C → Upper C); system starts in Lower C after reset
Switch 2 changes song in Auto-Play mode in round-robin order (Mary Had a Little Lamb → Twinkle Twinkle → Happy Birthday)
Octave selected in Piano mode carries over as the starting octave when entering Auto-Play mode
When song selection changes in Auto-Play mode, the new song must restart from the beginning immediately
System clock must be >16 MHz and <80 MHz
Must use interrupts for: (1) hardware timer and (2) push buttons/switches
Must use hardware timer(s) for timing control
At most one piano key may be pressed at a time (design assumption)
Hardware: TM4C123 LaunchPad, 4 push buttons, speaker + amplifier, resistors for 6-bit R-2R DAC
Optional: Add a fourth video game song as an option in Auto-Play mode
